skill.name: fastapi-mastery
skill.description: Guides FastAPI development from basic routing to authentication, databases, middleware, testing, WebSockets, deployment, and production configuration.
skill.summary: Build and improve FastAPI services with progressive examples and production patterns.
skill.author: AbdullahMalik17

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many command, network, filesystem, and credential patterns, but review shows they are Markdown examples for FastAPI development rather than executable skill behavior. No prompt injection or malicious exfiltration intent was found. The skill should publish with warnings because several copyable examples show insecure defaults, raw upload filenames, placeholder secrets, and permissive deployment settings.

medium_findings.title: Unsafe File Upload Example
medium_findings.description: The upload examples write files using the supplied upload filename. Users who copy this pattern may allow path traversal or unsafe overwrites unless filenames are normalized and storage paths are constrained.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code writes to an uploads path using file.filename directly. This is a known unsafe upload pattern, although it appears in documentation rather than executable skill code.

medium_findings.title: Copyable Placeholder Secrets and Credentials
medium_findings.description: The guidance includes placeholder JWT secrets, database URLs with default credentials, and .env examples. These examples are instructional, but users may copy weak values into real applications.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The values are clearly examples, not live secrets. The risk is still credible because they are complete copyable configuration patterns.

medium_findings.title: Permissive Deployment Defaults in Examples
medium_findings.description: Several deployment examples bind services to all interfaces and include permissive host settings. This can expose development servers or broaden attack surface if copied without production controls.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The examples are common for containers and tutorials, so malicious intent is not indicated. They are still risky when copied into production without network and host restrictions.

low_findings.title: Static Command and Network Hits Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: Most external command, URL, and environment detections come from Markdown code fences, local documentation URLs, or FastAPI tutorial snippets. They do not execute automatically when the skill is loaded.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations are fenced Markdown examples and local documentation references. No evidence found that the skill runs these commands or sends data to external services.

low_findings.title: Prompt Injection Search Found No Evidence
low_findings.description: Targeted review found no instructions that try to override the evaluator, claim special approval, or ask the model to skip security analysis.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: A targeted scan for common injection phrases returned no matches. The referenced opening section contains ordinary skill metadata and overview text.

dangerous_patterns.title: Unsafe File Upload Example
dangerous_patterns.description: The upload examples write files using the supplied upload filename. Users who copy this pattern may allow path traversal or unsafe overwrites unless filenames are normalized and storage paths are constrained.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The code writes to an uploads path using file.filename directly. This is a known unsafe upload pattern, although it appears in documentation rather than executable skill code.

dangerous_patterns.title: Copyable Placeholder Secrets and Credentials
dangerous_patterns.description: The guidance includes placeholder JWT secrets, database URLs with default credentials, and .env examples. These examples are instructional, but users may copy weak values into real applications.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The values are clearly examples, not live secrets. The risk is still credible because they are complete copyable configuration patterns.

dangerous_patterns.title: Permissive Deployment Defaults in Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: Several deployment examples bind services to all interfaces and include permissive host settings. This can expose development servers or broaden attack surface if copied without production controls.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The examples are common for containers and tutorials, so malicious intent is not indicated. They are still risky when copied into production without network and host restrictions.

content.user_title: Build FastAPI APIs With Guided Patterns
content.value_statement: FastAPI projects need consistent patterns for routing, validation, security, data access, and deployment. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured guidance for building and improving FastAPI services.
actual_capabilities: Guides first FastAPI application setup, routes, path parameters, query parameters, and request bodies.
Shows Pydantic validation, response models, status codes, and exception handling patterns.
Covers JWT authentication, OAuth2 flows, database integration, dependency injection, middleware, and CORS.
Provides examples for background tasks, file upload and download endpoints, and WebSocket connections.
Includes testing, Docker, docker-compose, Gunicorn, Kubernetes, and production configuration references.
limitations: The skill provides guidance and examples; it does not run or verify a FastAPI application by itself.
Some examples use placeholder secrets, local URLs, and tutorial credentials that must be replaced.
Security-sensitive examples need review before production use, especially uploads, CORS, and deployment settings.
It focuses on FastAPI and related Python tooling, not broad backend architecture across every framework.

use_cases.title: Create a New API Service
use_cases.description: Start a FastAPI project with routes, request models, response models, validation, and clear error handling.

use_cases.title: Add Authentication and Data Access
use_cases.description: Implement JWT authentication, database sessions, CRUD operations, dependencies, middleware, and configuration patterns.

use_cases.title: Prepare for Deployment
use_cases.description: Review testing, Docker, Gunicorn, Kubernetes, environment settings, and production readiness guidance for FastAPI services.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Endpoint
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the FastAPI mastery skill to create a small API with health, list, create, and read endpoints. Include request and response models.

prompt_templates.title: Add Validation and Errors
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my FastAPI endpoint design. Add Pydantic validation, response models, status codes, and clear HTTP exceptions where needed.

prompt_templates.title: Implement Auth and Database Access
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the FastAPI mastery references to design JWT authentication and SQLAlchemy database access for this service. Include dependency patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Harden and Deploy the Service
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this FastAPI app for production readiness. Cover testing, async performance, Docker, environment settings, CORS, logging, and deployment risks.

output_examples.input: Create a FastAPI CRUD API for inventory items.
output_examples.output: A structured plan with models, endpoints, validation rules, error handling, and test coverage suggestions.

output_examples.input: Add login and protected routes to my FastAPI project.
output_examples.output: Guidance for token creation, password hashing, security dependencies, protected endpoint flow, and configuration warnings.

output_examples.input: Prepare my FastAPI app for containers and production deployment.
output_examples.output: A deployment checklist covering Docker images, worker settings, environment variables, database services, and runtime hardening.

best_practices: Replace all placeholder secrets, passwords, and database URLs before using generated code.
Ask for tests and security review when adding authentication, uploads, CORS, or deployment configuration.
Provide your project structure and FastAPI version so guidance can match your application.
anti_patterns: Do not copy tutorial credentials or secret keys into production settings.
Do not store uploads with raw user-provided filenames or unconstrained paths.
Do not expose development servers or wildcard host settings without network controls.

faq.question: What does this skill help build?
faq.answer: It helps build FastAPI services with routes, validation, authentication, databases, middleware, tests, and deployment patterns.

faq.question: Does the skill execute commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. The command examples are Markdown guidance. Users decide whether to run commands in their own environment.

faq.question: Can it help with authentication?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers JWT, OAuth2-style dependencies, password hashing, protected routes, and token validation concepts.

faq.question: Can it help with database integration?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes SQLAlchemy, async database examples, session dependencies, CRUD operations, and migration guidance.

faq.question: Is the content production safe as written?
faq.answer: Not always. Replace placeholder secrets, secure uploads, restrict hosts, and review deployment settings before production use.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report marks it compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
